controller_design_notes: Same architecture as linear_regulator__baseline. After 1000 warmup batches, runs 500 batches of adversarial training against LinearDynamicsAdversary (ΔA·x state-matrix perturbation, 5 PGD inner steps per batch, eta_max=0.1 Frobenius projection).

resumption_strategy.mechanism: Full mid-run resumption via --resume. The training script saves an adversarial checkpoint every 100 batches (--checkpoint-every 100) to <output-dir>/checkpoints_adversarial/checkpoint_latest/ containing: model.eqx, adversary_<i>.eqx, ctrl_opt_state.eqx, adv_opt_state_<i>.eqx, and meta.json with batch_idx + loss histories + active-adversary index history. To resume: re-invoke train_minimax.py with the same flags plus --resume; the script loads warmup_model.eqx (skipping phase 1) and continues adversarial training from last_completed_batch + 1.
resumption_strategy.rng_caveat: Adversarial sampling RNG (key_adv) is NOT explicitly checkpointed; on resume, key_adv is re-derived from --seed and advanced via repeated jr.split to the resumed batch index. Produces a deterministic continuation given the same seed and last_completed_batch, but NOT bitwise identical to an uninterrupted run.
resumption_strategy.to_extend_beyond_500_batches: Re-run with --resume --n-adversary-batches <new_total> (e.g. 5000). The first 500 batches are loaded from the final checkpoint; training continues for the remaining 4500.

notes: Adversarial run for the corrected 4-model Δv comparison. Pairs with linear_regulator__baseline. Δv_regulator = (0.280 - 0.158) / 0.158 = +0.775 ± 0.034 (SEM, n=5) — adversarial training inflates peak velocity by ~78% relative to the baseline of the same architecture. Discriminator prediction (Δv_regulator > 0) supported. Adversary ΔA saturated the Frobenius projection at 0.1 (eta_max ceiling).
